pending.label: Add random book: Faulthurst's Asides I

content.title: Faulthurst's Asides I
content.author: Faulthurst
variants:
I've been counting the carriages.

Not for any productive reason. I lose track around eighty-something and start over. I think it gives me a sense of progress, even though I am, in objective terms, going nowhere.

If you find yourself doing the same, I would not call it foolish. I would call it human.

Someone once asked me whether the train ever stops.

I told them I'd never seen it stop. They asked if I'd ever felt it stop. I told them no. They asked if perhaps it had stopped right now and we simply couldn't feel it.

I have not slept well since.

The slimes are not, in any meaningful sense, evil.

They are slimes. They do what slimes do. They press themselves against you in the only way they know how, and the only way they know how happens to involve teeth.

It is not personal. Almost nothing on this train is personal, which is, in its own way, comforting.

pending.commit_message: content(narrative): add random book 'Faulthurst's Asides I'

pending.label: Add random book: Passenger Fragments I

content.title: Passenger Fragments I
content.author: Anonymous
variants:
Found this notebook on a bench between cars 47 and 48.

The handwriting is not mine. The dates inside don't match any calendar I recognise. But the entries describe this exact train.

I've decided to keep it.
The lanterns flicker differently in the carriages where the rails curve. I don't know if that means anything. I just know I notice it now.

Met a woman in the dining car who claimed she had been on the train for nine years. I asked how she kept track. She said she didn't. I asked how she knew it was nine.

She said someone told her.

pending.commit_message: content(narrative): add random book 'Passenger Fragments I'

pending.label: Add random book: Quiet Rules of the Train

content.title: Quiet Rules of the Train
content.author: Unknown
variants:
There are rules to this train, though no-one will tell you them.

Don't open a chest you've already opened.
Don't follow the same passenger for too long.
When the lanterns dim twice in a row, keep walking.

These are the rules I have made for myself.
They may not be yours.
But you will make your own, soon enough.

pending.commit_message: content(narrative): add random book 'Quiet Rules of the Train'

pending.label: Add story: Pip Aaro - The Waiting Child

letters.label: Letter One
variants:
My name is Pip. I am eight.

I am on a train I dont know. The seats are big. The seats smell like Grandmas coat. I dont know how I got here.

A man gave me this paper. Except he was not a man. He was a rat. A real rat. With a tail and everything. He wore clothes like a person.

He said when you need them youll know. I think he meant the paper. I am using it now so maybe I needed it now.

I am looking for Mum. She has brown hair. She is the kindest person you will ever meet.

I am a little bit scared. But only a little bit.

Pip

I was on a train with Mum. Then I fell asleep.

When I woke up Mum was not there. The train was different. The seats are different. The smell is different. The orange book I was reading is not in my bag.

I have a new bag now. I dont know whose bag it is.

A rat gave me this paper. He had a little vest on. He had a tail like a real rat does.

I am going to find Mum. I think she is just in a different carriage. There are so many carriages.

I am not crying. I have only cried a little bit.

Pip

letters.label: Letter Two
variants:
I asked a man where the conductor is.

He had a yellow coat. He laughed. Not in a mean way. He said sweetheart there is no conductor on this train.

He said the train does not stop.

I dont believe him. Trains stop. One time Mum and I waited a long time at a station because the lights were broken. Trains stop.

The man in the yellow coat is sleeping on his bench now. He sleeps a lot. I think he is sad.

I am not going to be like that.

Pip

I found a notebook under a seat.

Someone wrote in it for a long time. They wrote about counting carriages. They counted to 384 and then they stopped. They said there is no point looking for the front.

I dont know what they meant by the front. Trains have a front. Even this one has to.

I am keeping the notebook in my new bag. I will read it when I get sleepy. It is like having someone to talk to.

The person who wrote it sounded tired. I am not tired yet.

Pip

letters.label: Letter Three
variants:
Some of the people on the train are pretend.

They are not actually pretend. They look real. But they dont see you. I waved at a lady today and she walked right past me. I was right in front of her.

Mum says it is rude to ignore someone. So I think these people are not really there.

But some people ARE there. Like the man in the yellow coat. Like the rat. Like a lady who gave me an apple. The apple was a little brown but it was nice.

I am keeping a list of the real people. There are four real people so far. I think I am a real person too. Probably.

Pip

I made a plan today.

The plan was: walk one way for a long time. Dont turn around. Then I would be at the back of the train. Then I would know which way is forward.

I walked a really long time. I counted forty seven carriages. I did not turn around. I did not look back.

There was no back.

The carriage at the end looked like the carriage I started in. The same Grandma smell. I think it WAS the carriage I started in.

I dont understand. I want Mum.

Pip

letters.label: Letter Four
variants:
The lady with the apple is my friend now.

Her name is Soren. She wears the same sweater every day. Or maybe she has lots of the same sweater. I dont know which.

Soren has been on the train for a long long time. She wont say how long. She says counting is bad.

Soren asked about Mum. I told her about the brown hair and the toast and the orange book. Soren got quiet for a long time. Then she said your Mum sounds wonderful.

She said it the same way the man in the yellow coat said the train doesnt stop.

I think Soren is being kind. But I think she means something else too.

I am still going to find Mum.

Pip

There is a slime in my carriage. I named her Pebble.

Pebble is small and green. Soren said she is too small to be dangerous yet. I do not believe that about Pebble. Pebble is nice.

Pebble wobbles a lot. Sometimes she leaves a wet spot on the floor. You have to walk around it. She follows me to the door but she doesnt go through. I think she thinks the door is a wall.

Mum would say a slime is not a pet. Mum would say a slime is a monster. I think Mum is wrong about Pebble. I would tell Mum if I could.

I miss Mum a lot today.

But I have Pebble.

Pip

letters.label: Letter Five
variants:
Soren told me about the door.

She said there is a door on the train that goes outside. She said its in different places for different people.

She said if I find it I should go through it.

I thought about it for a really long time. I thought about Mum. I thought about Nan. I thought about Pebble who would be alone.

I think if Mum is on this train I have to keep looking. And if Mum is not on this train then she is somewhere I cant get to anyway.

So when I find the door I am going to sit next to it. I will wait there for Mum. Or for someone who knows where Mum is.

Soren said okay sweetheart. She did not say it the way she said it about Mum. She said it like she meant it.

Pip

I wrote a letter for Mum and I am going to leave it.

Soren said sometimes people pass through the same carriage many times. So if I leave the letter somewhere everyone goes maybe someone will give it to Mum.

The letter says my name is Pip Aaro. I am eight. I have brown hair like Mum. I have a friend called Soren and a slime called Pebble who is mostly safe.

The letter says I am okay. The letter says I am being brave.

The letter says I am waiting.

I think Mum will find it. Mums find things.

Pip

pending.commit_message: content(narrative): add story 'Pip Aaro - The Waiting Child'
